title: Conformance Known-Answer Vector File
description: Schema for conformance/vectors/*.json. NON-NORMATIVE: it validates testing artifacts, not CDX documents. Its job is to catch corruption or typos in a file that third parties consume, so the constraints are deliberately tight.

clause.description: The specification section these vectors pin.

oracle.description: How the expected values were derived. Required because a vector whose expectations came from the implementation under test proves nothing — it snapshots a bug instead of catching it.

requires.description: Capability keys (capabilities.json) that EVERY vector in this file needs — the file exercises a feature gated on them (e.g. the security extension, or provenance verification). Merged with each vector's own `requires`. Absent means the file is pure core and always in scope.

local.description: MUST NOT appear: `local` is this-implementation-only data and is stripped at the export boundary. Its presence means the boundary leaked.

requires.description: Capability keys (from capabilities.json) this vector needs. An adapter that does not declare all of them has the vector SKIPPED — reported explicitly, never counted as a pass. Absent means the vector is pure core and always in scope.

severity.description: Normative weight of this vector. Absent defaults to MUST: a failure is fatal. SHOULD failures are advisory and never fatal.
